title: How Endava is redesigning software delivery around AI agents
optimized_headline: Endava's Innovative Approach: Redesigning Software Delivery with AI Agents
raw_excerpt: Learn how Endava is using AI agents, ChatGPT Enterprise, and Codex to accelerate software delivery, automate workflows, and build an AI-native culture across the enterprise.
raw_body: Learn how Endava is using AI agents, ChatGPT Enterprise, and Codex to accelerate software delivery, automate workflows, and build an AI-native culture across the enterprise.
speedrun: Endava is reshaping its software delivery process by incorporating AI agents, including ChatGPT Enterprise and Codex. This integration aims to speed up workflows and foster an AI-centric culture within the company. By automating various tasks, they hope to enhance efficiency and productivity. This shift is significant as businesses increasingly look to AI for competitive advantages in software development.
why_it_matters: Endava's approach could streamline operations for software development teams, making their work faster and more efficient.
This move reflects a broader trend in the tech industry, where companies are increasingly adopting AI to enhance productivity and innovation.

lenses.eli12: Endava is using smart AI tools to make creating software faster and easier. Think of it like having a helpful assistant that takes care of routine tasks. This change could mean that people in tech spend more time on creative work instead of repetitive chores, benefiting everyone who relies on software.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, Endava's use of AI agents highlights a growing user need for efficiency in software development. By automating workflows, teams could reduce costs and deliver products faster. This could lead to quicker iterations and a better alignment with market demands.
lenses.engineer: From a technical standpoint, Endava is leveraging models like ChatGPT Enterprise and Codex to enhance software delivery. These AI agents can automate coding tasks and streamline communication within teams. While this can significantly improve productivity, engineers should remain aware of potential integration challenges with existing systems.
